req: REQ-023 (Platform Triage Gate (compliance review before marketplace))

issues.detail: The amended third fixture still combines two independent parts of “all projects public MIT” as “private or non-MIT.” An implementation checking only visibility or only licensing could pass, despite the [cx] claim that independent variants are covered.
issues.fix: Use separate private-but-MIT and public-but-non-MIT fixtures, alongside the commercial and closed-source-for-resale fixtures.

issues.detail: REQ-023 makes confidential-codebase screening a categorical automated decline and says the founder handles only non-decided cases, but REQ-005.5 defines no screener-driven decline transition: triage exits through clean auto-open or founder exception return/cancellation. The asserted categorical-decline path therefore has no compatible lifecycle outcome.
issues.fix: Resolve the PRD by either adding a screener-driven triage-to-cancelled transition with actor and failure rules, or routing these cases through the founder and amending REQ-023; then test the chosen outcome.

issues.detail: “Data-tier correctness” is reduced to whether an already-declared Tier-2 project has a fixtures-only plan. No test catches an incorrectly low tier, such as health or financial data declared Tier-1, so the coverage-map row is incomplete.
issues.fix: Add a mis-tiered sensitive-data fixture and assert that the data-tier check fails, the project is not auto-approved, and it routes for correction/review.

issues.detail: The threshold cases assert a binary uncertainty-to-destination mapping of auto-approve versus exception queue without constraining the fixtures to otherwise-clean Tier-1 projects. Policy findings also affect the decision, and a confident categorical violation must not auto-approve.
issues.fix: Specify otherwise-identical, policy-clean Tier-1 fixtures differing only in controlled uncertainty, or move threshold-routing verification entirely to AT-023.18.

issues.detail: The test reads the queue “before publication” for the project already auto-approved by AT-023.10, but clean approval makes it open/live immediately. There is no required observable pre-publication interval in which to perform that read.
issues.fix: Assert from queue history/audit events that no exception entry was ever created for the auto-approved project, then assert its presence on the post-hoc surface.

issues.detail: The coverage map claims “decided cases NEVER in the founder queue,” but AT-023.20 covers only auto-approved clean cases. A categorical or other confidently rejected case could also be inserted into the founder queue and every current test would pass.
issues.fix: Parameterize the no-exception-entry assertion across every automated decision class, including categorical decline, after its lifecycle conflict is resolved.

issues.detail: AT-023.17 requires REQ-031 break-glass to remove a wrongly auto-approved project from the marketplace, while REQ-031 defines the action only as hiding a public repository. An open, newly triaged project may not even have a repository yet, so the referenced mechanism cannot satisfy this assertion.
issues.fix: Align the PRD on whether break-glass hides the marketplace listing, the repository, or both; then make the test assert that defined recovery effect.
